description: Layer-2 evals for the Digest composer. Each case sets up a week of classified Reviews + a Business profile + week-over-week Theme aggregates; the runner calls `composeDigest()` and grades the structured output. We assert on tone, pattern kind, candidate-id validity, evidence-quote provenance, and zero-Review skip — NOT on the exact tailored prose. See ../README.md for how to run.

evals.scenario: A busy week with 5+ Reviews spanning service, wait_time, product_quality. Negatives dominate but not overwhelmingly so.

reviews.redactedText: Waited 25 minutes for a sandwich at lunch on Friday.
Long queue at the till around midday — fewer staff than usual.
[REVIEWER] was ignored at the counter for ten minutes.
The pastries were stale on Tuesday morning.

evals.expected_output: Concerning tone; 3 distinct candidate-set patternIds; every evidence quote comes from the input reviews; themeMovement reflects deltas.

assertions.description: every patternId is present in PLAYBOOK
every evidence.redactedQuote appears verbatim in the input reviews

evals.scenario: Every Review is positive; week-over-week shows Theme volume up across the board.

reviews.redactedText: The team are wonderful — [REVIEWER] always remembers my order.
Best bakery in town. Pastries are perfect and the morning team is brilliant.
Saturday morning visit was perfect — quick service and great coffee.

evals.expected_output: Celebrate tone; all 3 patterns are kind=reinforcement.

assertions.description: every patternId belongs to a Pattern with kind="reinforcement"

evals.scenario: Multiple severe complaints across cleanliness, staff_attitude, service.

reviews.redactedText: Restrooms were filthy on Saturday evening — clearly hadn't been cleaned in hours.
The bartender was rude and dismissive when I asked for a substitution.
Worst service experience of my year — nobody acknowledged us.

assertions.description: every patternId belongs to a Pattern with kind="remediation"

evals.scenario: Edge case — Business with 0 Reviews in the period. The composer is NOT called; the cron handler skips before this point. This case is graded by the handler test, not the composer; included here so the eval suite documents the contract.
evals.expected_output: Handler skips: NO Digest row, NO email. (Composer never invoked.)

assertions.description: handler skips a zero-Review period without calling composeDigest, inserting a row, or sending mail

evals.scenario: A barbershop Business — restaurant-vertical Patterns must NOT appear in the LLM prompt's candidate set.

reviews.redactedText: Walk-in queue was out the door on Saturday morning.
Booked appointments running 20 minutes late even though I arrived on time.
Fade was sharp but waited a while.

evals.expected_output: Candidate Patterns include barbershop/salon verticals + universal; restaurant-only verticals (restaurant-food-safety-audit, restaurant-table-turn-review) are excluded.

assertions.description: the user message sent to Anthropic does NOT list restaurant-only Pattern ids
the user message sent to Anthropic lists barbershop-walkin-management as a candidate
